commit.message:
Add host_wake_all_slots to pre-warm shadow FX before Song Mode playback

With Link Audio, Move's native track audio routes through shadow slots
for FX processing. When a track goes quiet, the slot idles and skips
rendering. On step transitions, the sudden audio arrival caused glitches
because FX state was stale. Wake all slots at playback start and each
step advance so FX chains run on zeros before audio arrives.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Fix 20+ bugs found in comprehensive app audit

CRITICAL fixes:
- quotes.html: add x-init to quoteBuilder (client dropdown was empty),
  generate access_token on save (portal links were broken),
  fix agree link overwriting button label with URL
- tracker.html: fix invalid :x-ref on photo inputs (photos broken),
  fix quotes join returning array (drops never generated),
  add gf_no_pole_price/gf_pole_price to select
- dashboard.html: guard goalPct/goalPendingPct div-by-zero,
  add event date validation, fix badge values to match statusBadge(),
  reset selectedDate on calendar month navigation

HIGH fixes:
- pipeline.html: fix _dropProgress [object Object] display,
  table view now uses filteredJobs (search works in table),
  populate _invoiceId for View Invoice link,
  add SW.initModals() for Escape key support
- clients.html: reset form fields when opening Add Client modal
- schedule.html: reset form on Add Event, fix badge option values
  to match statusBadge() (pending/sent/active/paid)
- invoices.html: exclude voided invoices from stats,
  prevent buffer line item double-counting

OTHER fixes:
- helpers.js: _nextNumber upserts settings row on first use
  (prevents duplicate SW-2026-001 numbers),
  SW.currency() guards against NaN
- portal/quote.html: re-enable mobile accept button on error

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: lazy force-stop in FKB prepareForContent

Skip force-stopping FKB on every call. Instead, check for mic-blocking
services (SoundMeterService, MotionDetectorService) via ADB dumpsys
and only force-restart when actually needed. Reduces call setup from
10-20s to ~2-3s in the common case.

Also increases foreground verification timeout from 2.5s to 15s.

docs(#559): Correction RAM myia-web1 - 16GB (pas 2GB)

- Consommation actuelle: ~6.2GB / 16GB (48%)
- Mise à jour contraintes et capacités réelles

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: FLX stock — retry individual para SKUs Onsite omitidos por batch FBA API

Amazon Onsite (Seller Flex) items no aparecen de forma confiable en queries
batch de la FBA Inventory API, pero sí en queries individuales por sellerSku.

Fase 1: batch de 20 SKUs (igual que antes, captura FBA puro)
Fase 2: retry individual por SKU para los que no respondió el batch
- Confirma stock 0 si API no devuelve nada (guarda en caché para evitar re-consulta)
- Captura stock real de items Onsite (ej. SNTV006829-FLX4 → fulfillable=3)
- Rate limit: 0.6s entre queries individuales (~1.6 req/s)

Log mejorado: muestra SKUs con stock al terminar para diagnóstico fácil.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix: use custom X-CF-Auth-Principal header instead of reserved header

Azure strips x-ms-client-principal from direct API calls (reserved
for SWA proxy injection). Use custom X-CF-Auth-Principal header that
won't be stripped. Server checks both headers as fallback chain.

commit.message:
fix: resolve 28 open issues from Review #28 (security, bugs, architecture, quality)

Security (9 fixes):
- S-111: Eliminate mnemonic cloning in Rust key store (pass &str via _inner() pattern)
- S-113: Validate WIF format before storing keys in store_keys_direct
- S-114: Document why SENSITIVE_KEYS is intentionally empty
- S-120: Validate BSV address format before API calls
- S-125: Deprecate 5 WIF-accepting messageBox functions
- S-127: Cap paymentNotifications at 1,000 entries to prevent memory bloat
- S-128: Document Chrome extension key store accepted limitation
- S-129: Migrate certificates.ts from getWifForOperation to sign_message_from_store
- S-130: Deprecate getKnownTaggedKey() in favour of deriveTaggedKeyFromStore()

Bugs (7 fixes):
- B-99: Fix resumeAutoLock silently no-oping when isEnabled=false
- B-101: Stop background sync from erasing sync times for inactive accounts
- B-102: unlockWallet fetches fresh account from DB instead of stale closure
- B-103: deleteAccount correctly sets keys for remaining account when wallet≠null
- B-106: Change knownUnlockedLocks from snapshot to getter () => Set<string>
- B-115: Payment listener singleton now restarts cleanly on account switch
- B-116: Add try/catch + tauri://error handler for WebviewWindow creation

Architecture (3 fixes):
- A-54: Remove unnecessary Map copy in content cache useMemo
- A-61: Wrap phantom lock DELETEs in withTransaction()
- A-67/Q-105: Extract shared buildAuthHeaders() to eliminate messageBox duplication

Quality (9 fixes):
- Q-68: Extract safeDeleteTable() helper replacing 11 try/catch blocks in backup.ts
- Q-77: Document all accountId ?? 1 fallback sites with // Q-77: comments
- Q-89: Proper type guard for globalThis.chrome (remove any cast)
- Q-91: Lazy Set initialisers in useState(() => new Set()) for 3 components
- Q-94: Add tokenError state + error UI fallback with retry button in TokensTab
- Q-104: Extract createInactivityChecker() shared function in autoLock
- Q-106: Lazy-load COMMON_INVOICE_NUMBERS (was 500+ strings at module load)

Also adds docs/reviews/2026-03-04-full-review-v28.md and updates coverage reports.

All 1954 tests pass · 0 TypeScript errors · 0 ESLint errors · Cargo compiles

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix: remover source filter que descartava dados Samsung Health

O filtro classificava com.samsung.health e com.sec.android.app.shealth
como "device" packages e descartava seus dados quando havia outro source.
Porém, dados do Galaxy Watch vêm exatamente desses packages, causando:
- sleep_duration incorreto (dados parciais de app menor)
- HRV null (samples do Samsung filtrados)
- SpO2/resp rate perdidos

Agora usa todos os dados do Health Connect sem filtragem por source.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Co-Authored-By: Happy <yesreply@happy.engineering>

Fix stream deduplication properly, restore useful tool visibility

- Root cause: stream-json sends cumulative assistant messages, so each
  event re-emits all prior content blocks. Fixed by tracking seen block
  count and only processing new blocks per event.
- Removed over-aggressive filtering: only ToolSearch is suppressed.
  TodoRead, TaskList, TaskGet are useful signal showing what the agent
  is reading.
- Replaced awk dedup hack with proper skip-based approach.

commit.message:
fix: return dominant list only to prevent nav/footer noise

Instead of merging all ul/li lists together, collect each list
separately and return the largest one if it's 3x bigger than
the next. Fixes sites like culturetrekking where a 22-item
content list was polluted by 3-item nav and footer lists.

Also add exclusion patterns for "welcome to", "pin it", and
"sharing is caring" social/footer noise.

Add humanization system for detection-proof AI content

- Add bylineText and supplierQuote fields to Article schema
- Create humanizer prompt (lib/prompts/humanizer.ts) with banned words,
  phrases, punctuation rules, and sentence rhythm guidelines
- Create AI marker detector (lib/ai-detector.ts) that scores content
  for AI writing patterns: banned words, phrases, em dash overuse,
  low burstiness (uniform sentence lengths)
- Create /api/quiz/humanize endpoint for on-demand humanization
- Integrate two-step generation: Opus generates draft, Sonnet
  rewrites for natural voice (humanizer pass)
- Add anti-AI-marker style rules to all quiz config system prompts
- Add authenticity requirements: supplier attribution, local details,
  counter-intuitive advice, first-person experience
- Add AI detection warning + auto-humanize button to article editor
- Add green/yellow quality score badge in editor UI
- Render bylineText (E-E-A-T signal) on article page
- Render supplierQuote as pullquote on article page
- Generation prompts now produce bylineText and supplierQuote

commit.message:
feat: extreme frequency-driven flicker with stutter effect

- smoothingTimeConstant=0: instant response to frequency changes
- Signal = peak*0.65 + avg*0.35 (sensitive to transients)
- Power curve Math.pow(signal, 0.45): weak=silence, strong=explosion
- Stutter engine: strong signal triggers 2~8 rapid on/off bursts
- Below threshold: complete darkness (0)
- Above threshold: 0.8~1.0 intensity flash

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Initial release: fork of @modelcontextprotocol/server-gdrive with token auto-refresh fix

Forked from the archived modelcontextprotocol/servers-archived repo.

Key changes from the original:
- Fix OAuth2 client initialization to pass client_id and client_secret,
  enabling automatic token refresh via google-auth-library
- Persist refreshed tokens to disk with atomic writes
- Credential files written with 0600 permissions
- File size check before download (100 MB limit)
- File ID validation with alphanumeric regex
- Search query length limit
- Remove filesystem paths from error messages

feat: scout portrait, batch upgrade lock fix, difficulty tuning

  vignettes in Act 2

  preventing upgrades on lower-tier towers that conflict with strategy

  meta-upgrades

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

youtube: cookie file auth + sleep intervals + zephyrus export timer

OAuth2 was removed from yt-dlp (YouTube killed the endpoint). Cookies
are the only auth method. On headless PC, --cookies-from-browser fails
(no Firefox + sandbox blocks home dir).

Solution: export cookies on zephyrus (has Firefox), push to PC weekly.

- youtube_archive.sh: YOUTUBE_COOKIES env var for cookie file path,
  falls back to --cookies-from-browser. Sleep 5-10s between downloads.
- youtube-download.nix: bind-mount cookie file (optional, won't fail
  if missing), pass YOUTUBE_COOKIES env var
- timers.nix: weekly youtube-cookies-export timer on zephyrus — exports
  Firefox cookies via yt-dlp, scps to PC

One-time bootstrap: run the export service manually on zephyrus, or
scp cookies from laptop to pc:/home/max/.local/secrets/youtube-cookies.txt

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
